programme.title: PL STORIES EP34: (Subbed) CESC FABREGAS
programme.sub-title: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.
programme.desc: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP46: (Subbed) PHILIPPE COUTINHO
programme.sub-title: We take a look at the career of Philippe Coutinho, tracing his journey from a promising young talent to one of the Premier League’s most skilful and dynamic players. We join Coutinho at his current club Vasco da Gama and reflect on his time in Europe, from his rise at Liverpool to his time at Barcelona, Bayern Munich and Aston Villa. Delving into his passion, creativity, and legacy, we celebrate how his flair and vision have left a lasting mark on the Premier League.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP04: JOHN TERRY HALL OF FAME SPECIAL
programme.sub-title: Considered a legend at Stamford Bridge, John Terry won no fewer than five League titles at Chelsea as well as lifting the F.A. Cup five times and the Champions League trophy. We find out what set him apart from his peers and where he rates amongst the very best in the World.
programme.desc: Considered a legend at Stamford Bridge, John Terry won no fewer than five League titles at Chelsea as well as lifting the F.A. Cup five times and the Champions League trophy. We find out what set him apart from his peers and where he rates amongst the very best in the World.

programme.title: 1-TO-11 EP11: PAUL KONCHESKY
programme.sub-title: Paul Konchesky, the tenacious and hardworking left-back who graced the Premier League with clubs like West Ham, Charlton, Tottenham and Leicester City, joins Will Perry for an unmissable episode of 1-to-11. With 347 top-flight appearances under his best, Konchesky reflects on his illustrious career to select his ultimate line-up of teammates. Expect bold choices a surprising inclusion, and a formation designed to deliver one thing… goals, goals, goals. A fascinating insight into the mind of one of football’s most dependable defenders.
programme.desc: Paul Konchesky, the tenacious and hardworking left-back who graced the Premier League with clubs like West Ham, Charlton, Tottenham and Leicester City, joins Will Perry for an unmissable episode of 1-to-11. With 347 top-flight appearances under his best, Konchesky reflects on his illustrious career to select his ultimate line-up of teammates. Expect bold choices a surprising inclusion, and a formation designed to deliver one thing… goals, goals, goals. A fascinating insight into the mind of one of football’s most dependable defenders.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP08: (Subbed) SERGIO AGUERO HALL OF FAME SPECIAL
programme.sub-title: He’s fondly remembered for grabbing the last gasp goal that won Manchester City their first Premier League title back in 2012, and with 184 goals in just 275 appearances it clear to see why Sergio Aguero has been inducted into the hall of fame. We talk to him and his former teammates to reflect on an incredible career that saw him win 5 Premier League titles, 1 F.A. Cup and 6 League Cups.
programme.desc: He’s fondly remembered for grabbing the last gasp goal that won Manchester City their first Premier League title back in 2012, and with 184 goals in just 275 appearances it clear to see why Sergio Aguero has been inducted into the hall of fame. programme.title: PL STORIES EP38: (Subbed) AARON RAMSEY
programme.sub-title: After a meteoric rise through Cardiff’s youth system, Aaron Ramsey’s dream of playing for one of the world’s biggest clubs was realised. But just as his career was reaching new heights, a devastating double leg break threatened to bring it all crashing down. The Wales captain takes us through his footballing journey to date.
programme.desc: After a meteoric rise through Cardiff’s youth system, Aaron Ramsey’s dream of playing for one of the world’s biggest clubs was realised. But just as his career was reaching new heights, a devastating double leg break threatened to bring it all crashing down. The Wales captain takes us through his footballing journey to date.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP37: (Subbed) FERNANDINHO
programme.sub-title: Never one to showboat or grab headlines, Fernandinho still managed to make a colossal impact at Manchester City and the Premier League. Follow the story of ‘little Fernando’, having returned to his Brazilian boyhood club, as he tells the story of his childhood, playing in Ukraine, and the effect of featuring in one of the most iconic Brazil defeats of all time.
programme.desc: Never one to showboat or grab headlines, Fernandinho still managed to make a colossal impact at Manchester City and the Premier League. programme.title: PL STORIES EP05: MANAGER RIVALRIES
programme.sub-title: We peel back the layers on the Premier League’s most iconic managerial rivalries; battles that shaped eras, defined clubs, and captivated fans around the world. From the fierce mind games between Arsène Wenger and Sir Alex Ferguson, to the emotional explosion of Kevin Keegan’s title-chasing Newcastle. From the simmering tension and touchline clashes of Mourinho vs. Wenger, to the tactical chess match between Jürgen Klopp and Pep Guardiola. Through exclusive interviews and unforgettable moments, we explore how these rivalries pushed the limits and left a lasting mark on the Premier League.

programme.sub-title: A West Ham cult hero, Slaven Bilic first represented the Irons as a player in the 1990s. He returned to the club as manager in 2015, ahead of the Hammers’ final season at Upton Park. The Croatian looks back on what proved to be a remarkable campaign in East London.

programme.sub-title: Jermain Defoe scored 162 Premier League goals, and it was at Tottenham where he was at his prolific best. The striker recalls his two spells with the North London club, including the memorable occasion where he hit 5 in one match.

With insight, passion and a few surprises, PL Moments offers a personal look at the journey of the league’s all-time top scorer.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP42: (Subbed) NEURODIVERSITY CELEBRATION
programme.sub-title: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.
programme.desc: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP39: KICKS AND CULTURE STATESIDE
programme.sub-title: The Premier League is seeing a big boost in popularity amongst the Black American population and we travelled Stateside to learn more about this growing appeal. The show is led by Aaron Dolores, football fan and founder of Black Arrow, a creative movement on a quest to promote the intersection of football and black culture, not just in the US but worldwide. He takes us on a journey where we meet varying characters from the Mayor of Birmingham Alabama, Randall Woodfin, former players Tim Howard and Jozy Altidore, to current player Chris Richards as well as authentic Black American fans immersed in Premier League culture.
programme.desc: The Premier League is seeing a big boost in popularity amongst the Black American population and we travelled Stateside to learn more about this growing appeal. The show is led by Aaron Dolores, football fan and founder of Black Arrow, a creative movement on a quest to promote the intersection of football and black culture, not just in the US but worldwide. He takes us on a journey where we meet varying characters from the Mayor of Birmingham Alabama, Randall Woodfin, former players Tim Howard and Jozy Altidore, to current player Chris Richards as well as authentic Black American fans immersed in Premier League culture.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP06: RODRI & LAMPARD
programme.sub-title: Arguably the best player in his position in world football right now, Rodri has honed his talents on the pitch to help Manchester City achieve a record 4 Premier League titles in a row. In this week’s Big Interview, former Chelsea and City playmaker, Frank Lampard talks to Rodri about the intricacies of his game. From positioning, breaking up play to how he’s developed his role, this midfield masterclass will reveal all about his game.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP51: GOODBYE GOODISON
programme.sub-title: As the curtain comes down on Everton’s time at Goodison Park, PL Stories takes a poignant look back at one of English football’s most iconic grounds. For over 130 years, Goodison has been more than just a stadium; it’s been a home, a fortress, and the stage for countless moments of triumph, heartbreak, and unforgettable drama. Through the voices of players, fans, and historians, we relive the stories etched into the blue brick walls; from title-winning glory to relegation battles, Merseyside derbies to European nights. This is a tribute to the legacy of Goodison Park, and the soul of a club that has lived and breathed within its stands.
programme.desc: As the curtain comes down on Everton’s time at Goodison Park, PL Stories takes a poignant look back at one of English football’s most iconic grounds. For over 130 years, Goodison has been more than just a stadium; it’s been a home, a fortress, and the stage for countless moments of triumph, heartbreak, and unforgettable drama. Through the voices of players, fans, and historians, we relive the stories etched into the blue brick walls; from title-winning glory to relegation battles, Merseyside derbies to European nights. This is a tribute to the legacy of Goodison Park, and the soul of a club that has lived and breathed within its stands.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP15: TIM CAHILL
programme.sub-title: With over 200 appearances for Everton, Tim Cahill established himself as the high-energy midfielder who helped drive the side. For PL Stories this week, Cahill talks through his journey to the Premier League from battling his way to make it as a footballer in his native Australia, leaving home as a teenager and adapting to English life, scoring in a World Cup as well as the difficult decision to leave Goodison Park after 8 years.
programme.desc: With over 200 appearances for Everton, Tim Cahill established himself as the high-energy midfielder who helped drive the side. For PL Stories this week, Cahill talks through his journey to the Premier League from battling his way to make it as a footballer in his native Australia, leaving home as a teenager and adapting to English life, scoring in a World Cup as well as the difficult decision to leave Goodison Park after 8 years.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP50: CHRIS WOOD
programme.sub-title: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. Back in his hometown, we meet the family, coaches and friends who helped shape the player and the person he’s become. From humble beginnings to Premier League mainstay, Wood reflects on the pressures he's faced, the pride of leading his country, and the mindset that's driven him every step of the way.

programme.title: PL MASTERCLASS EP05
programme.sub-title: Some of the Premier League's biggest stars take us onto the pitch to discuss the techniques and thought processes that allow them to perform at the highest level. Featuring Morgan Rogers, Joelinton, Martin Odegaard and Ryan Gravenberch.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP06: RODRI & LAMPARD
programme.sub-title: Arguably the best player in his position in world football right now, Rodri has honed his talents on the pitch to help Manchester City achieve a record 4 Premier League titles in a row. In this week’s Big Interview, former Chelsea and City playmaker, Frank Lampard talks to Rodri about the intricacies of his game. From positioning, breaking up play to how he’s developed his role, this midfield masterclass will reveal all about his game.

For over 130 years, Goodison has been more than just a stadium; it’s been a home, a fortress, and the stage for countless moments of triumph, heartbreak, and unforgettable drama. Through the voices of players, fans, and historians, we relive the stories etched into the blue brick walls; from title-winning glory to relegation battles, Merseyside derbies to European nights. This is a tribute to the legacy of Goodison Park, and the soul of a club that has lived and breathed within its stands.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP49: BILL FOLEY
programme.sub-title: Upon creating an American Ice Hockey team, owner Bill Foley outlandlishly claimed his team would lift the Stanley Cup in six years. His dream came true. Now, owner of AFC Bournemouth and flying in the Premier League, he’s already making bold claims for the Cherries’ future.
programme.desc: Upon creating an American Ice Hockey team, owner Bill Foley outlandlishly claimed his team would lift the Stanley Cup in six years. His dream came true. Now, owner of AFC Bournemouth and flying in the Premier League, he’s already making bold claims for the Cherries’ future.

programme.title: Review of the Season 2024/2025 (LIV)
programme.sub-title: Relive another extraordinary Premier League campaign as Liverpool soared to an historic 20th league title, Nottingham Forest defied expectations and the promoted sides were strenuously challenged. There were also tactical transformations, shifts in narrative trends, and a more open field than ever before. Review of the Season documents all of the thrilling stories to emerge across the 380 matches.
programme.desc: Relive another extraordinary Premier League campaign as Liverpool soared to an historic 20th league title, Nottingham Forest defied expectations and the promoted sides were strenuously challenged. There were also tactical transformations, shifts in narrative trends, and a more open field than ever before. Review of the Season documents all of the thrilling stories to emerge across the 380 matches.

programme.title: UNDER THE SKIN NOTTINGHAM FOREST
programme.sub-title: This documentary celebrates the incredible, rollercoaster ride of Nottingham Forest Showcasing the club’s fans, culture and traditions, we see how Forest’s return to European football was achieved by reconnecting with the legacy of Brian Clough.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP05: MANAGER RIVALRIES
programme.sub-title: We peel back the layers on the Premier League’s most iconic managerial rivalries; battles that shaped eras, defined clubs, and captivated fans around the world. From the fierce mind games between Arsène Wenger and Sir Alex Ferguson, to the emotional explosion of Kevin Keegan’s title-chasing Newcastle. From the simmering tension and touchline clashes of Mourinho vs. Wenger, to the tactical chess match between Jürgen Klopp and Pep Guardiola. Through exclusive interviews and unforgettable moments, we explore how these rivalries pushed the limits and left a lasting mark on the Premier League.
programme.desc: We peel back the layers on the Premier League’s most iconic managerial rivalries; battles that shaped eras, defined clubs, and captivated fans around the world. From the fierce mind games between Arsène Wenger and Sir Alex Ferguson, to the emotional explosion of Kevin Keegan’s title-chasing Newcastle. From the simmering tension and touchline clashes of Mourinho vs. Wenger, to the tactical chess match between Jürgen Klopp and Pep Guardiola. Through exclusive interviews and unforgettable moments, we explore how these rivalries pushed the limits and left a lasting mark on the Premier League.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP43: (Subbed) FOXES FANTASY WORLD
programme.sub-title: After watching their already relegated side lose to the Invincibles on the last day of the 2003/4 Premier League season, some Leicester City fans stayed behind to watch Arsenal lift the trophy at Highbury. Those fans endured ten years in the wilderness, only for Leicester to return to the big time and embark on a rollercoaster couple of years. In that time they completed the great escape after being dead and buried at the foot of the table and then followed that up with the most unthinkable of Premier League triumphs. This is their story, one of sports greatest triumphs, told by those who wrote it.
programme.desc: After watching their already relegated side lose to the Invincibles on the last day of the 2003/4 Premier League season, some Leicester City fans stayed behind to watch Arsenal lift the trophy at Highbury. Those fans endured ten years in the wilderness, only for Leicester to return to the big time and embark on a rollercoaster couple of years. In that time they completed the great escape after being dead and buried at the foot of the table and then followed that up with the most unthinkable of Premier League triumphs. programme.title: 1-TO-11 EP11: PAUL KONCHESKY
programme.sub-title: Paul Konchesky, the tenacious and hardworking left-back who graced the Premier League with clubs like West Ham, Charlton, Tottenham and Leicester City, joins Will Perry for an unmissable episode of 1-to-11. With 347 top-flight appearances under his best, Konchesky reflects on his illustrious career to select his ultimate line-up of teammates. Expect bold choices a surprising inclusion, and a formation designed to deliver one thing… goals, goals, goals. A fascinating insight into the mind of one of football’s most dependable defenders.
